translation_status.review_note: EN is native. RU is human-reviewed (originals or human-revised). KO and VI translations were produced by an LLM (the agent on this branch) and have not been read by a native speaker. All KO/VI translations pass automated script-consistency tests (no Cyrillic in KO/VI bodies, no Hangul in VI/RU bodies, no 'kanji' substring in VI), which catch only the most egregious LLM artifacts — semantic accuracy and natural phrasing still need a human pass before paid promotion. This applies uniformly to all 7 articles now: the original 2 (PR #273/#276/#277) and the 5 new ones migrated 2026-07-21 (PR feature/blog-migrate-5-drafts).

claims.claim: Anki added FSRS support in 23.10 (October 2023) and enabled it by default for new collections in 24.10+.
claims.note: Inline citation present in all 4 locales. Wording differentiates 23.10 introduction vs 24.10 default-on for new collections.

claims.claim: Anki desktop is free and open source; AnkiDroid free; AnkiWeb sync free; AnkiMobile iOS is the only paid piece.
Origa uses FSRS spaced repetition.
Origa imports .anki2, .anki21, .anki21b decks.
Origa has 200,000+ native phrases.
Origa runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and web; iOS is planned but not shipping.
Origa OCR and STT models run locally (offline card creation).
Origa interface languages: English, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean.
WaniKani is radical-based, English interface, paid subscription.
Bunpro is grammar-focused SRS, web-based, English interface.
Duolingo is gamified and shallow for Japanese.
Migii is a JLPT test simulator (timed practice).
manga-ocr (kha-white) — open-source library, multi-line manga OCR.
Poricom (blueaxis) — desktop GUI for manga-ocr.
YomiNinja (matt-m-o) — pipes OCR output into Yomitan/Yomichan.
KanjiSnap (iOS) — uses Apple Live Text for OCR.
Imiwa, Nihongo — iOS/Android dictionaries with OCR.
2136 joyo kanji — MEXT official count.
ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, Gemini Advanced — ~$200/year (~$17/month) each, mid-2026 pricing.
sources: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google public pricing pages

claims.claim: Speak ~$30/month Premium Plus; Talkpal ~$20/month Premium; Praktika ~$100/year.
Minna no Nihongo, Genki, Tae Kim's Guide — real Japanese textbooks.
sources: publisher sites, Tae Kim's Guide GitHub

flags: KO and VI translations are machine_translated (LLM). Script-consistency tests guard against the most common LLM artifacts (cross-script hallucination), but native-speaker review is required before paid promotion.

5 articles (learn-japanese-from-manga, japanese-ocr-app, best-japanese-learning-app-offline, japanese-ai-tutor, yaponskiy-s-nulya) migrated 2026-07-21 from marketing/blog/ drafts. Source drafts verified by external research specialist (round-2 factcheck).
gate_reason: All 7 articles ship with verified competitor claims and inline citations. FSRS wording verified against docs.ankiweb.net. EN/RU are human-quality (originals or human-revised); KO and VI are LLM translations marked in translation_status — eligible to ship (script-consistency verified, content structure sound) but a human review pass is recommended before any paid promotion or rich-result eligibility push on KO/VI markets. The 6th draft (ru-anki-alternativa-yaponskiy) was deferred due to SEO cannibalization with anki-alternative-japanese; decision tracked in uwuwu/origa-blog-anki-cannibalization-decision.

brand_voice_check.competitor_disparagement: none — every competitor has a 'when it is best' framing
